fileName: MCLN EX92-Cigarette Diversion Barcode Configuration Guide.docx
webUrl: MCLN EX92-Cigarette Diversion Barcode Configuration Guide.docx
title: MCLN EX92-Cigarette Diversion Barcode Configuration Guide
fullText:
MCLN EX92 Configuration Guide Enablement T o enable EX92 : Cigarette Diversion within an environment, first global mods must be enabled. This is located within System Codes UI code C-000 and should be configured as below. Second, EX92 specific enablement must also be configured and turned on. First create Code ID EX92, Description EX92 Extension Control within the C-000 system code. Second, create a parameter 1-1 with description Enablement Flag that will contain the enablement switch itself if it does not already exist . Finally, ensure it is turned on. Barcode/Work Area Configuration C-92A: Barcode Equivalency Within the System Codes UI, create a new custom code with Code Type 92A and Code ID EQV. This custom code will contain the barcode equivalency information. This allows certain work areas to be considered equivalent to others for the purposes of applying a diversion barcode. Parameters can be added to the Code ID from the Parameters option at the bottom right. Sample parameter configuration will vary based on the desired work areas, but a sample is shown below. A completed parameter list will look something like the following. In this example, where a CIGP work area exists, it will be treated as though it were CIGU. Note that t his will not affect any fields or flows apart from barcode reference field update . C-92B: Barcode/Work Area Configuration Within the System Codes UI, create a new custom code with Code Type 92 B and Code ID 92B . This custom code will contain the work areas required for an oLPN in order to apply a barcode as well as the barcode values themselves. Parameters can be added from the Parameters option at the bottom right. P arameter configuration will vary based on the desired work areas, but a sample is shown below. Note that the distance between the From and To positions here will dictate the maximum length of the resulting Value. The below, from 1 to 4, will allow for a maximum four character value. A completed parameter list will look something like the following. Required work areas are held in no particular order in the parameter description with commas used as delineators, and the barcode that will be applied is held in the corresponding parameter value. Note that WM OS performs no data verification on these fields ; they will be implemented exactly as configured. In this example, if an oLPN is created that results in a picking task from work areas CIGU and CIGH, barcode value 004 will be applied to the reference field. C-92 C : CIGU Allocation Type Barcodes Within the System Codes UI, create a new custom code with Code Type 92C and Code ID 92C. This custom code will contain allocation types in work area CIGU that are required for an oLPN in order to apply a barcode as well as the barcode values themselves. Description name: ‘ EX92C CIGU Allocation Type Barcodes ’ Parameters can be added from the Parameters option at the bottom right. Parameter configuration will vary based on the desired allocation types , but a sample is shown below. Note that the distance between the From and To positions here will dictate the maximum length of the resulting Value. The below, from 1 to 9 , will allow for a maximum 9 character value. A completed parameter list will look something like the following. Required allocation types are held in no particular order and the barcode that will be applied is held in the corresponding parameter value. Note that WMOS performs no data verification on these fields; they will be implemented exactly as configured . C-92C: CIGU Allocation Type Data Setup Users can assign a llocation types by utilizing the WaveAllocationType tag within the LineItem element of the XML Sample Flowthrough In this example, the created Cigarette Pick task results in picks from location CU66542. This location is configured with Work Area CIGU. There are no barcode equivalencies in 92A for CIGU- CIGP will be considered as CIGU, and this will not affect our task shown above in any case. In 92B, Work Area CIGU is associated with barcode reference value 002. As a result, reference field LPN.REF_FIELD_11 is populated with barcode reference value 002.

A completed parameter list will look something like the following. In this example, where a CIGP work area exists, it will be treated as though it were CIGU. Note that t his will not affect any fields or flows apart from barcode reference field update . A completed parameter list will look something like the following. Required work areas are held in no particular order in the parameter description with commas used as delineators, and the barcode that will be applied is held in the corresponding parameter value. In this example, if an oLPN is created that results in a picking task from work areas CIGU and CIGH, barcode value 004 will be applied to the reference field.
